Class.Documentation:
This class manages the order entities in the Cool Supplies application

This class contains methods to pay order and penalty order and cancel order

@author  Suthiesan Subramaniam
Class.Name: CoolSuppliesFeatureSet10Controller

Parameter.Documentation_plain: the authorization code for payment

Operation.Documentation:
A Helpeer method that determines if a bundle item is eligible to be includedin an orderbased on the order's purchase level.This method checks if the item's level (Mandatory, Recommended, or Optional)matches the purchase level of the order. The eligibility criteria are asfollows:- If the order level is "Mandatory", only items with the "Mandatory" levelare included.- If the order level is "Recommended", both "Mandatory" and "Recommended"items are included.- If the order level is "Optional", all items are included regardless oftheir level.

@author Mary Li
Operation.Documentation_plain: A Helpeer method that determines if a bundle item is eligible to be includedin an orderbased on the order's purchase level.This method checks if the item's level (Mandatory, Recommended, or Optional)matches the purchase level of the order. The eligibility criteria are asfollows:- If the order level is "Mandatory", only items with the "Mandatory" levelare included.- If the order level is "Recommended", both "Mandatory" and "Recommended"items are included.- If the order level is "Optional", all items are included regardless oftheir level. @author Mary Li
Operation.Name: isLevelEligibleForOrder
Operation.ReturnTypeDocumentation: true if the item's level is eligible for inclusion in the order, false otherwise
Operation.ReturnTypeDocumentation_plain: true if the item's level is eligible for inclusion in the order, false otherwise

Parameter.Documentation: the purchase level of the order (Mandatory, Recommended, or Optional)
Parameter.Documentation_plain: the purchase level of the order (Mandatory, Recommended, or Optional)
Parameter.Name: orderLevel

Parameter.Documentation: the purchase level of the item to be checked
Parameter.Documentation_plain: the purchase level of the item to be checked
Parameter.Name: itemLevel

Operation.Documentation:
Retrieves the details of a specific order by its index.This method handles both regular items and GradeBundle items.For GradeBundle items, it adds each eligible bundle item separately andappliesa discount if more than one item is included from the bundle.

@author Mary Li, Zhengxuan Zhao
Operation.Documentation_plain: Retrieves the details of a specific order by its index.This method handles both regular items and GradeBundle items.For GradeBundle items, it adds each eligible bundle item separately andappliesa discount if more than one item is included from the bundle. @author Mary Li, Zhengxuan Zhao

Operation.Documentation:
Retrieves the details of all orders in the system.This method handles both regular items and GradeBundle items.For GradeBundle items, it adds each eligible bundle item separately andappliesa discount if more than one item is included from the bundle.Each order is returned as a TOOrder object containing its associated itemsand total price after applying any discounts.

@author Mary Li, Zhengxuan Zhao
Operation.Documentation_plain: Retrieves the details of all orders in the system.This method handles both regular items and GradeBundle items.For GradeBundle items, it adds each eligible bundle item separately andappliesa discount if more than one item is included from the bundle.Each order is returned as a TOOrder object containing its associated itemsand total price after applying any discounts. @author Mary Li, Zhengxuan Zhao

Class.Documentation:
Gherkin step definitions that are related to adding bundle items in grade bundle.

The following class contains the step definitions required for the gherkin scenarios

related to adding bundle items to a grade bundles.

Co-authored by many team members. All the methods in the class were co-authored by the

listed authors.

@author Arthur Huang

@author Maëlle Anguenot

@author Bora Denizasan

@author Hakim Bekkari

Parameter.Documentation: The name of the grade bundle from which the admin is attempting to retrieve the item.Example:<pre>When the school admin attempts to get a bundle item with name "pencil"from a grade bundle with name "Bundle 5" (p11)</pre>If the grade bundle exists, it will filter and retrieve the item by its name. If thegrade bundle does not exist, the retrieved items list is cleared.
Parameter.Documentation_plain:
The name of the grade bundle from which the admin is attempting to retrieve the item.Example:
When the school admin attempts to get a bundle item with name "pencil"from a grade bundle with name "Bundle 5" (p11)
If the grade bundle exists, it will filter and retrieve the item by its name. If thegrade bundle does not exist, the retrieved items list is cleared.
Parameter.Name: bundleName

Parameter.Documentation: The name of the grade bundle from which the admin is attempting to retrieve all bundle items.Example:<pre>When the school admin attempts to get all bundle items from a grade bundlewith name "Bundle 5" (p11)</pre>If the grade bundle exists, it retrieves all the items associated with that bundle. Ifthe grade bundle does not exist, the retrieved items list is cleared.
Parameter.Documentation_plain:
The name of the grade bundle from which the admin is attempting to retrieve all bundle items.Example:
When the school admin attempts to get all bundle items from a grade bundlewith name "Bundle 5" (p11)
If the grade bundle exists, it retrieves all the items associated with that bundle. Ifthe grade bundle does not exist, the retrieved items list is cleared.
Parameter.Name: bundleName

Operation.Name: the_number_of_bundle_item_entities_in_the_system_shall_be_p11
Operation.ReturnTypeDocumentation: @throws AssertionError if the actual number of bundle items does not match the expected number.
Operation.ReturnTypeDocumentation_plain: @throws AssertionError if the actual number of bundle items does not match the expected number.

Parameter.Documentation: The expected number of bundle items in the system, provided as a string. Example:<pre>When the school admin attempts to get a bundle item with name "pencil" from a grade bundle with name "Bundle 5" (p11)Then the number of bundle item entities in the system shall be "4" (p11)</pre>The method asserts that the size of the bundleItems list equals the expected number.
Parameter.Documentation_plain:
The expected number of bundle items in the system, provided as a string. Example:
When the school admin attempts to get a bundle item with name "pencil" from a grade bundle with name "Bundle 5" (p11)Then the number of bundle item entities in the system shall be "4" (p11)
The method asserts that the size of the bundleItems list equals the expected number.
Parameter.Name: expectedNumber

Operation.Name: the_following_bundle_item_entities_shall_be_presented_p11
Operation.ReturnTypeDocumentation: @throws AssertionError if any of the expected items is not found among the retrieved bundle items.
Operation.ReturnTypeDocumentation_plain: @throws AssertionError if any of the expected items is not found among the retrieved bundle items.

Parameter.Documentation: The Cucumber data table containing the expected bundle item entities with properties such as quantity, level, gradeBundleName, and itemName.Example:<pre>When the school admin attempts to get all bundle items from a grade bundle with name "Bundle 5" (p11)Then the number of bundle item entities in the system shall be "4" (p11)Then the number of bundle item entities for grade bundle "Bundle 5" in the system shall be "3" (p11)Then the following bundle item entities shall be presented (p11)| quantity | level       | gradeBundleName | itemName ||        3 | Mandatory   | Bundle 5        | pencil   ||        2 | Optional    | Bundle 5        | eraser   ||        1 | Recommended | Bundle 5        | textbook |</pre>The method asserts that for each expected item, an item with matching quantity, level,gradeBundleName, and itemName is found in the list of retrieved bundle items.
Parameter.Documentation_plain:
The Cucumber data table containing the expected bundle item entities with properties such as quantity, level, gradeBundleName, and itemName.Example:
When the school admin attempts to get all bundle items from a grade bundle with name "Bundle 5" (p11)Then the number of bundle item entities in the system shall be "4" (p11)Then the number of bundle item entities for grade bundle "Bundle 5" in the system shall be "3" (p11)Then the following bundle item entities shall be presented (p11)| quantity | level       | gradeBundleName | itemName ||        3 | Mandatory   | Bundle 5        | pencil   ||        2 | Optional    | Bundle 5        | eraser   ||        1 | Recommended | Bundle 5        | textbook |
The method asserts that for each expected item, an item with matching quantity, level,gradeBundleName, and itemName is found in the list of retrieved bundle items.
Parameter.Name: dataTable

Operation.Name: the_number_of_bundle_item_entities_for_grade_bundle_in_the_system_shall_be_p11
Operation.ReturnTypeDocumentation: @throws AssertionError if the actual number of items does not match the expected number.
Operation.ReturnTypeDocumentation_plain: @throws AssertionError if the actual number of items does not match the expected number.

Parameter.Documentation: The name of the grade bundle.
Parameter.Documentation_plain: The name of the grade bundle.
Parameter.Name: bundleName

Parameter.Documentation: The expected number of bundle items in the grade bundle. Example:<pre>When the school admin attempts to get all bundle items from a grade bundle with name "Bundle 5" (p11)Then the number of bundle item entities in the system shall be "4" (p11)Then the number of bundle item entities for grade bundle "Bundle 5" in the system shall be "3" (p11)</pre>If the grade bundle exists, it checks the actual number of items in the bundle. If thegrade bundle does not exist, it checks that the expected number is 0.
Parameter.Documentation_plain:
The expected number of bundle items in the grade bundle. Example:
When the school admin attempts to get all bundle items from a grade bundle with name "Bundle 5" (p11)Then the number of bundle item entities in the system shall be "4" (p11)Then the number of bundle item entities for grade bundle "Bundle 5" in the system shall be "3" (p11)
If the grade bundle exists, it checks the actual number of items in the bundle. If thegrade bundle does not exist, it checks that the expected number is 0.
Parameter.Name: expectedNumber

Class.Documentation:
Step definition class for the "Update Student" feature. This class defines the Gherkin steps used

in the feature file and connects them with the underlying system logic to update students.

@author Alexander Kudinov
Class.Name: UpdateStudentStepDefinitions

Attribute.Documentation:
Gherkin step definition for updating a student in the system. The school admin attempts to

update a student by specifying the current name, the new name, and the new grade level.

@param currentStudentName The current name of the student.

@param newStudentName The new name to update the student to.

@param newStudentGradeLevel The new grade level of the student.
Attribute.Name: coolSupplies

Operation.Documentation: Gherkin step definition to ensure that a student with a specified name and grade level does notexist in the system after an update operation.
Operation.Documentation_plain: Gherkin step definition to ensure that a student with a specified name and grade level does notexist in the system after an update operation.
Operation.Name: the_student_with_grade_level_shall_not_exist_in_the_system_p4

Parameter.Documentation: The name of the student.
Parameter.Documentation_plain: The name of the student.
Parameter.Name: studentName

Parameter.Documentation: The grade level of the student.
Parameter.Documentation_plain: The grade level of the student.
Parameter.Name: studentGradeLevel

Operation.Documentation: Gherkin step definition to ensure that a specific list of student entities exists in thesystem. The method checks if the expected students, including their names and grade levels, arepresent in the system.
Operation.Documentation_plain: Gherkin step definition to ensure that a specific list of student entities exists in thesystem. The method checks if the expected students, including their names and grade levels, arepresent in the system.
Operation.Name: the_following_student_entities_shall_exist_in_the_system_p4

Parameter.Documentation: A DataTable containing the expected student names and grade levels.
Parameter.Documentation_plain: A DataTable containing the expected student names and grade levels.
Parameter.Name: dataTable
